definition: A visual representation of the frames and bindings used to evaluate a program; shows how names map to values
A record of local variable bindings created by a function call; part of the environment used during evaluation
A technique where a function calls itself on a smaller subproblem until reaching a base case
Separating how compound data is used from how it is constructed; access is via constructors and selectors
A style organized around objects that bundle data with methods that operate on that data; supports inheritance and polymorphism
An object that produces a sequence of values one at a time; supports the __iter__ and __next__ protocol in Python
A function that yields values lazily using the `yield` keyword
column3: producing an iterator that resumes between calls

definition: The process of evaluating expressions in a language by walking the abstract syntax tree; alternative to compilation
